summary: Security groups. Rule changes are security-relevant — guardrailed with a second approver; public CIDRs are rejected deterministically.

operations.title: Add an internal ingress rule
operations.summary: Open inbound access on one port from an internal address range. Public ranges are blocked.
operations.description: Add one ingress rule from an internal (RFC1918) CIDR. Security-relevant — guardrailed. Public CIDRs and 0.0.0.0/0 are rejected before submit.

params.name: security_group
params.label: Security group
params.help: Security group that receives the new ingress rule, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed security group can be opened up.

params.name: protocol
params.label: Protocol
params.help: IP protocol the rule allows; the allowlist permits only tcp and udp, so a rule authored here can never grant all-protocols (-1) access.

params.name: from_port
params.label: From port
params.help: First port of the range being opened; 1-65535 is the valid TCP/UDP port space and excludes 0, so a rule cannot silently mean all ports.

params.name: to_port
params.label: To port
params.help: Last port of the range being opened (set equal to From port for a single port); 1-65535 is the valid TCP/UDP port space, keeping the opening to an explicit, bounded range.

params.name: cidr_block
params.label: Source CIDR

bounds.semantic: internal cidr only — RFC1918, no public range, no 0.0.0.0/0

params.help: e.g. 10.0.0.0/16 — internal only.

operations.title: Remove an ingress rule
operations.summary: Close an existing inbound rule on this security group. Reversible, it can be re-added, and a second approver signs off.
operations.description: Remove an existing ingress rule from a security group. Reversible (it can be re-added), guardrailed with a second approver.

params.name: security_group
params.label: Security group
params.help: Security group containing the ingress rule to remove, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed security group can be edited.

params.name: rule_id
params.label: Rule to remove
params.help: The rule key/description to remove.

operations.title: Toggle revoking all rules on group delete
operations.summary: Set whether deleting this group also revokes all its rules first. Governs the blast radius of a future delete; an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Set the boolean `revoke_rules_on_delete` argument on a security group. When true, Terraform revokes all ingress and egress rules before deleting the group. Engineer-only: this governs the blast radius of a future delete (explicit HIGH criterion). Default is false.

params.name: security_group
params.label: Security group
params.help: The security group to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: revoke_rules_on_delete
params.label: Revoke rules on delete
params.help: true = revoke all ingress/egress rules before the group is deleted.

operations.title: Add an ingress rule sourced from another security group
operations.summary: Open inbound access on one port from members of another security group.
operations.description: Add one ingress (inbound) rule whose source is an existing security group (sets the ingress block's `security_groups`), scoped to a tcp/udp port range. Security-relevant — guardrailed. The source is bounded to security groups already in inventory (doc: "A group name can be used relative to the default VPC. Otherwise, group ID"); no CIDR is involved.

params.name: security_group
params.label: Security group
params.help: The security group to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: source_security_group
params.label: Source security group
params.help: An existing security group whose members become the allowed inbound source.

params.name: protocol
params.label: Protocol
params.help: Protocol to use; #{protocol} preserves the client's incoming protocol.

params.name: from_port
params.label: From port
params.help: tcp/udp port (1-65535).

params.name: to_port
params.label: To port
params.help: tcp/udp port (1-65535).

operations.title: Add a self-referencing ingress rule
operations.summary: Let members of this security group reach each other inbound on one port.
operations.description: Add one ingress (inbound) rule whose source is the security group itself — the op sets the ingress block's `self = true` (doc: boolean) — scoped to a tcp/udp port range. Lets members of the group reach each other inbound. Guardrailed; no external source is involved. self=false would be a sourceless rule, so it is fixed to true.

params.name: security_group
params.label: Security group
params.help: The security group to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: protocol
params.label: Protocol
params.help: Protocol to use; #{protocol} preserves the client's incoming protocol.

params.name: from_port
params.label: From port
params.help: tcp/udp port (1-65535).

params.name: to_port
params.label: To port
params.help: tcp/udp port (1-65535).

operations.title: Add an egress rule destined to another security group
operations.summary: Open outbound access on one port to members of another security group.
operations.description: Add one egress (outbound) rule whose destination is an existing security group (sets the egress block's `security_groups`), scoped to a tcp/udp port range. Guardrailed. Destination bounded to security groups already in inventory (doc: "A group name can be used relative to the default VPC. Otherwise, group ID"); no CIDR is involved.

params.name: security_group
params.label: Security group
params.help: The security group to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: destination_security_group
params.label: Destination security group
params.help: An existing security group that becomes the allowed outbound destination.

params.name: protocol
params.label: Protocol
params.help: Protocol to use; #{protocol} preserves the client's incoming protocol.

params.name: from_port
params.label: From port
params.help: tcp/udp port (1-65535).

params.name: to_port
params.label: To port
params.help: tcp/udp port (1-65535).

operations.title: Add a self-referencing egress rule
operations.summary: Let members of this security group reach each other outbound on one port.
operations.description: Add one egress (outbound) rule whose destination is the security group itself — the op sets the egress block's `self = true` (doc: boolean) — scoped to a tcp/udp port range. Lets members of the group reach each other outbound. Guardrailed; no external destination is involved. self=false would be a destinationless rule, so it is fixed to true.

params.name: security_group
params.label: Security group
params.help: The security group to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: protocol
params.label: Protocol
params.help: Protocol to use; #{protocol} preserves the client's incoming protocol.

params.name: from_port
params.label: From port
params.help: tcp/udp port (1-65535).

params.name: to_port
params.label: To port
params.help: tcp/udp port (1-65535).

operations.title: Create a new security group
operations.summary: Create a new security group for a tier or service, with an optional starter rule. An engineer finalizes and reviews it before it applies.
operations.description: Create a security group for a new tier or service, optionally with one bounded starter rule — more rules ride the existing rule actions afterwards. Submitting routes the complete specification to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: group_name
params.label: Group name
params.help: Shown wherever traffic rules are chosen.

params.name: description
params.label: What this group is for
params.help: AWS requires a description; make it the plain answer to what traffic policy this is.

params.name: vpc
params.label: Network (VPC)
params.help: Which network the group lives in.

params.name: first_rule_protocol
params.label: First inbound rule — protocol
params.help: Optionally open one port range now; add more later with the rule actions.

params.name: first_rule_from_port
params.label: First inbound rule — from port
params.help: First port of the range being opened — the same bounded port space as every rule form.

params.name: first_rule_to_port
params.label: First inbound rule — to port
params.help: Last port of the range; set equal to the from port for a single port.

params.name: first_rule_cidr
params.label: First inbound rule — source

bounds.semantic: internal cidr only — RFC1918, no public range, no 0.0.0.0/0

params.help: e.g. 10.0.0.0/16 — internal ranges only; public ranges are never self-service.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Group name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this group holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this group.

decisions: Confirm the outbound rules — the estate default allows all outbound traffic
Attaching the group to servers or services happens per resource afterwards
